programs.name: Michigan Choice Waiver Program
programs.what_it_does: This Medicaid-based program provides comprehensive long-term support services in participants' homes as an alternative to nursing home placement, serving about 18,000 seniors statewide. Local agencies coordinate individualized care plans, with a 'Self-Determination' option allowing seniors to select their own caregivers, often adult children (though not spouses or guardians).[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors requiring nursing home level of care but wishing to stay at home; must qualify for Medicaid.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific Medicaid waiver with self-determination feature enabling payment to family caregivers in most cases, not universally available in other states.
programs.benefit_value: Home-based long-term care services for ~18,000 participants; paid family caregiving option.

programs.name: MI Health Link Program
programs.what_it_does: This program integrates Medicare and Medicaid services into a single managed care plan for dual-eligible individuals, streamlining healthcare delivery and eliminating copays or deductibles for in-network care including prescriptions. It reduces out-of-pocket costs for participants in 25 participating counties.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible seniors age 21+ in 25 MI counties with Medicare Parts A/B/D and full Medicaid, not in hospice, MI Choice Waiver, or PACE.
programs.whats_unique: Michigan-specific integrated care model for dual eligibles, designed by state health department to simplify separate federal systems.
programs.benefit_value: No copays/deductibles on covered care; serves thousands of older residents.

programs.name: MI Options
programs.what_it_does: This statewide program connects older adults and people with disabilities to free person-centered counseling for long-term care, Medicare, home-based services like meal delivery or in-home care. It helps navigate benefits, services, and community supports through a network of local partners.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults and people with disabilities in Michigan seeking long-term care or Medicare guidance.
programs.whats_unique: State-launched counseling network by MDHHS specifically to simplify Michigan's long-term care and Medicare options.
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling and connections to home/community services.

programs.name: Senior Project Fresh Program
programs.what_it_does: This seasonal supplemental nutrition program provides eligible older adults with benefits to exchange for locally-grown produce and honey at Michigan farmers' markets and roadside stands. It promotes access to fresh, local foods.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Michigan-specific state program focused on redeeming benefits at local MI farmers' markets for fresh produce/honey.
programs.benefit_value: Seasonal vouchers for fresh Michigan produce (value varies by eligibility).

programs.name: MiCAFE (Michigan's Coordinated Access to Food for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: This program assists seniors with benefit applications to access food assistance via Michigan Bridge Card for grocery purchases. It helps overcome barriers like complex paperwork or transportation to offices.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ statewide, including those who may think they have too much income to qualify.
programs.whats_unique: Statewide application assistance program by Elder Law of Michigan tailored for elderly food benefits.
programs.benefit_value: $23-$292/month in food benefits for single seniors.

programs.name: Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: Volunteers aged 55+ provide assistance and friendship to adults with difficulty in daily activities, helping them maintain independence. It complements core aging services through Michigan's aging network.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Adults needing help with daily living; volunteer providers 55+.
programs.whats_unique: State-implemented program within Michigan's aging network for senior volunteers aiding peers.
